June 18, 1990
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 rrJrr Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 958L4
Request for opinion, Possible Conflict of Interest
Gentlemen:
f request an opinion as to the following issue:
DOES A CONTRACT NEGOTIATED BY AN AGENT FOR A DEVELOPMENT
COMPANY WITH A MEMBER OF THE CITY COUNCIL INVOLVING THAT
COUNCIL MEMBER'S PRIVATE PROPERTY CONSTITUTE A CONFLICT OF
TNTEREST SUCH AS TO BAR PARTICIPATION IN A DECISION INVOLVING
THE AGENT WHEN ACTING FOR HIS OWN INTERESTS IN SEEKING HELP
FROM THE CTTY ON A COUPLETELY DIFFERENT PROJECT?
During the past year, I had extensive negotiations with a
development company involving a piece of real estate owned by
me being annexed to another city. A formal agreement was
signed on August 21, 1989.
Most of those negotiations were conducted between the
developer and rne by the developer's agent, Sidney Kibrick.
The negotiations resulted in an aqreement between the
developer and me whereunder the developer has agreed to
ttswap'r land with me, if the city of Rancho Mirage (the city
to which my property was annexed) makes ingress to my
property from a public way impossible. The contract, with
reasonable foreseeability, has sufficient value to constitute
a material financial effect on my property under the
provisions of FPPC Regulation L8702.3.
The contract, though negotiated by the agent, Mr. Kibrick,
hras signed by Federated Development Company officials, 
and
not Mr. Kibrick. I have no formal or inforrnal agreement with
Mr. Kibrick personally.
However, I have stated to friends and associates infornally
that I feel indebted to Mr. Kibrick for having facilitated
negotiations that could have been much less successful.
Mr. Kibrick is now seeking public assistance, either from the
Cathedral City City Council or from the Redevelopment Agency
of the city of Cathedral City, oD which I also serve as a
I
i.,
P.O, BOX 5OOl . 68-625 PEREZ ROAD . CATHEDRAL CITY, CA 92235-5OOl . 619 / 324-8388 . FAX: 619 / 324-4816
I


result of tny position on the city council, for a developnent
which his own separate development company is pursuing in the
city of cathedral city. Federated Developnent company, the
principal in the negotiations which led to the contractual
agreement, is in no vay involved in this new proposal.
Sorne menbers of the pubtic, and one associate on the city
council, have suggested that I should abstain on the subject
of Mr. Kibrick's ner^, proposal, based on at least the
appearance of, and possibility of an actual, conflict of
interest. Since I have received no actual consideration fron
Mr. Kibrick personally, or fron the development conpany he is
now representing. I question vhether I an legally required to
abstain from participation. Although my comments about I'being
indebted to Mr, Kibrick'r night prove enbarrassing, I know
that I cannot IegaIIy or properly use ny public position to
repay "favorsl Lrhich I may deem to be olred to others. If I
participate in the decision, rny decision wiII be based on the
nerits on1y, and not because of any perceived 'robligationl.
The request by ur. Kibrick at this time involves a
request for public assistance in putting in a public
road to property being acquired by him more than a nile
distant fron any property owned by me. It will have no
rnaterial financial effect on ne or on any property owned by
me, in ny opinion. However, the agreenent which Ur. Kibrlck
negotiated vith rne in his capacity as agent for Federated
Development cornpany (and which resulted in a signed
agreement les6 than a year ago) would almost certainly be
regarded as having had a material financiaL effect on ny
property, under the provisions of Section 18702.3, of the
FPPC regulations.
Although Hr. Kibrick was not hinself the source of that
financial effect, the developnent conpany he vas then
representing was.
f r^rouLd appreciate your advice on whether the financial
effect of the princi.pal's action wiII preclude rne fron acting
on the pending request by that development conpany's then
spokestnan and agent, acting solely in his private capacity in
the present instance. I have provided a copy of ny contract
lrith Federated Development company to the city attorney,
A1len R. Briggs, rrho is authorized to forrrard a copy to you
if you so request. You nay contact hin at (619) 770-0363.

sections.question: DOES A CONTRACT NEGOTIATED BY AN AGENT FOR A DEVELOPMENT COMPANY WITH A MEMBER OF THE CITY COUNCIL INVOLVING THAT COUNCIL MEMBER'S PRIVATE PROPERTY CONSTITUTE A CONFLICT OF INTEREST SUCH AS TO BAR PARTICIPATION IN A DECISION INVOLVING THE AGENT WHEN ACTING FOR HIS OWN INTERESTS IN SEEKING HELP FROM THE CITY ON A COMPLETELY DIFFERENT PROJECT?
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a city council member must abstain from voting on a development proposal by an agent (Sidney Kibrick) who previously negotiated a valuable land-swap contract for the council member with Kibrick's former employer (Federated Development Company), when Kibrick is now seeking assistance from the city council for his own separate development project.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter to FPPC, not an FPPC response letter. No CONCLUSION or SHORT ANSWER section is present. The document contains a clearly stated QUESTION in all caps. City council member writing from Cathedral City requesting FPPC opinion regarding potential conflict of interest involving prior contractual dealings with an agent now seeking city assistance. Document quality is poor (OCR artifacts visible), but core content is extractable.

embedding.summary: A Cathedral City council member requests FPPC guidance on whether prior negotiations conducted by Sidney Kibrick (on behalf of Federated Development Company) regarding a valuable land-swap contract affecting the council member's property creates a conflict of interest that would bar the council member from participating in votes on Kibrick's current separate development project in the city.
